title: Outdoor Grill Prep Cart
summary: A rolling counter-height cart with a slatted cedar top and full lower shelf, sized for a portable grill.
description:
A portable grill needs a place to actually prep food next to it — this cart is that surface, built to counter height (30") and mounted on casters so it rolls wherever the grill goes.

Both the top and the lower shelf are **slatted cedar boards with gaps between them**, not solid panels. That's deliberate: a solid wood top right next to a hot grill traps moisture and cups; slats let air move and water drain instead of pooling.

Length and width both adjust from the numbers here — widen or shorten the frame to match your own grill's footprint before you cut the center supports and slats.

tools.note: Or a miter saw for the crosscuts.

cutList.note: Adjustable — widen to fit your grill.
Adjustable — shorten to fit your space.

steps.title: Cut the legs and drill pocket holes
steps.body: Cut the four 4x4 legs to 23-3/4" and drill pocket holes in both ends of every 2x4 piece.

steps.title: Build the two end-frame assemblies
steps.body: Join the short (21") width aprons between pairs of legs with pocket screws to build two identical end frames. This sets the cart's width — adjust this measurement now if your grill needs more or less room.

steps.title: Join the end frames
steps.body: Join the two end frames with the long (65") length aprons to form the full-length frame.

steps.title: Add the center supports
steps.body: Add the 2x4 center supports (24-1/2") across the frame — these are what carry the top and shelf slats, so space them to keep any gap between slats from sagging under a loaded platter.

steps.title: Install the top and shelf slats
steps.body: Cover the top and lower shelf openings with the cedar slats, spaced evenly with a consistent gap between each — the gap is what lets water drain and air circulate instead of pooling next to a hot grill.

steps.title: Add casters and finish
steps.body:
Attach the four caster wheels to the bottom of the legs, then finish the whole cart with an exterior penetrating stain.

Use a heat-safe surface under the grill itself and follow the grill manufacturer's own clearance instructions — this cart is a prep surface, not a heat shield.
